attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- analyze-data: Analyze local data files (Excel, CSV) with statistical methods, anomaly detection, trend analysis, and visualization. Generates structured Markdown reports with charts. Use when user wants to analyze data, explore a dataset, find anomalies, or create a data report from a local file. Trigger: 'analyze data', 'Daten analysieren', 'Excel analysieren', 'analyze Excel', 'data analysis', 'Anomalien finden', 'Datenanalyse', 'explore data', 'Daten auswerten', 'Datei analysieren', 'CSV analysieren', 'analyze-data'.
- company-intel: Research companies for B2B Account Based Marketing and personalized email outreach. Identifies buying signals (hiring, funding, leadership changes), technology stack, strategic initiatives, and personalization triggers. Generates actionable intelligence optimized for sales teams. Use when user wants company intelligence for B2B sales, ABM campaigns, or personalized cold outreach. Trigger: 'company intelligence', 'research company for outreach', 'ABM research', 'company intel', 'sales intelligence', 'account research', 'B2B research', 'Firmen Intelligence', 'Unternehmensrecherche für Verkauf'.
- content-creator: Create, update, and manage blog content with AI-powered research, writing, and image generation. Stores results in Supabase. Three commands: 'create' (new article from topic/URL/text), 'update' (edit existing article), 'regenerate-image' (new image for existing article). Use when user wants to create blog content, write articles, update existing content, or regenerate article images. Trigger: 'create content', 'neuer Artikel', 'Blog schreiben', 'Content erstellen', 'Artikel aktualisieren', 'update content', 'Bild regenerieren', 'regenerate image', 'content-creator'. For batch creation from research, use content-orchestrator instead.
- content-orchestrator: Orchestrate the full content pipeline: research trending topics, curate with AI assessment and human decision-making, then batch-create articles with review. Combines content-research and content-creator into one conversational workflow with Human-in-the-Loop. Includes run-based archiving and context-window-resilient state tracking. Use when user wants to run the full content pipeline, curate researched topics, or batch-create multiple articles from research. Trigger: 'content orchestrator', 'Content Pipeline', 'Themen kuratieren', 'Pipeline starten', 'content-orchestrator', 'Orchestrator'. For research only, use content-research instead. For single article creation, use content-creator instead.
- content-publisher: Publish content from Supabase to Webflow (blog) and LinkedIn (posts). Supports immediate publishing via API and scheduled/queued publishing via Edge Function. Use when user wants to publish a blog post, post to LinkedIn, schedule content, or manage the publish queue. Trigger: 'publish content', 'Blog publizieren', 'auf Webflow publizieren', 'LinkedIn posten', 'content publisher', 'content-publisher', 'Artikel veröffentlichen', 'schedule post', 'Post einreihen', 'publish queue', 'Publishing Status'. For content creation, use content-creator instead.
- content-research: Research trending topics via web search and YouTube API. Generates a curated list of 10-20 topics as Markdown file with overview table and details. Configurable for any topic area via topic files. Use when user wants to research trending topics, find viral content ideas, or generate a topic list for content planning. Trigger: 'content research', 'topic research', 'trending topics', 'virale Themen', 'Content Recherche', 'Themen recherchieren', 'content-research'. For full pipeline with curation and batch creation, use content-orchestrator instead.
- convert-word: Convert Markdown files to professionally formatted Word documents (.docx) with Roboto font and Swiss standard margins. Use when user wants to create a plain (unbranded) Word document from Markdown. For Callista-branded output, use convert-word-callista instead. Trigger: 'convert to Word', 'create docx', 'export as Word', 'Markdown to Word', 'Word document', 'Word erstellen'.
- convert-word-callista: Convert Markdown files to Callista-branded Word documents (.docx) with Montserrat headings, Callista header/footer, and company address. Use ONLY when user explicitly wants Callista branding. For plain (unbranded) Word output, use convert-word instead. Trigger: 'Callista Word', 'Callista docx', 'branded Word document', 'Callista document', 'Callista Dokument'.
- monthly-summary: Generate structured monthly Markdown summaries by reading Outlook email and calendar data. Supports configurable profiles (sales, recruiting, etc.) stored as .txt files. Use when user wants a monthly report or summary from Outlook data. Trigger: 'monthly summary', 'Monatszusammenfassung', 'sales summary', 'monthly report', 'Monatsbericht', 'monthly-summary'. For raw Outlook access, use outlook skill instead.
- n8n-setup: Install, configure, and maintain n8n-mcp MCP server and n8n-skills for Claude Code. Covers security hardening (telemetry disable, tool restrictions, SSRF protection), MCP config in mcp.json, skill installation, updates, and troubleshooting. Use when user wants to set up, update, or troubleshoot the n8n integration with Claude Code. Trigger: 'n8n setup', 'n8n installieren', 'n8n-mcp konfigurieren', 'n8n update', 'n8n-mcp updaten', 'n8n skills installieren', 'n8n troubleshooting'.
- nanobanana: Generate professional images using Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image) or Nano Banana Pro (Gemini 3 Pro Image) via OpenRouter API. Model switchable via NANO_BANANA_MODEL in .env. Four styles: 'infographic' for technical diagrams, system architectures, and data visualizations; 'metaphoric' for symbolic, cinematic imagery; 'youtube-thumbnail' for click-optimized YouTube video thumbnails with 5 sub-styles (photorealistic, bold-graphic, minimalist, data, illustrated); 'sketchnote' for hand-drawn visual sketch notes of talks, podcasts, concepts, and books with 5 layout types (talk-summary, concept-explainer, process-journey, comparison, book-summary). Use when user wants to create images, diagrams, visualizations, YouTube thumbnails, or visual sketch notes. Trigger: 'create image', 'generate infographic', 'make a visual', 'Bild erstellen', 'Infografik', 'nanobanana', 'YouTube thumbnail', 'Thumbnail erstellen', 'Video Bild', 'sketch note', 'Sketchnote', 'visual notes', 'visuelle Notizen'.
- outlook: Manage Outlook via Microsoft Graph API. Read emails, download/add attachments, create draft replies, read calendar, create events, read/create contacts. NEVER sends emails — all outgoing communication saved as drafts only. Use when user wants to interact with Outlook, Office 365, mailbox, calendar, or contacts. Trigger: 'check email', 'read inbox', 'neue Emails', 'Termin erstellen', 'Kalender', 'Kontakt suchen', 'Email beantworten', 'outlook'.
- pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- youtube-transcript: Fetch YouTube video transcripts via Supadata API. Returns timestamped or plain-text transcripts in 100+ languages. Supports saving to file. Use when user wants to read, extract, or analyze a YouTube video transcript. Trigger: 'YouTube transcript', 'Transkript', 'Video transkribieren', 'youtube-transcript', 'YT transcript', 'Untertitel abrufen'.
- shipwright-project:project: Decomposes project requirements into well-scoped planning units for /shipwright-plan. Generates CLAUDE.md and agent_docs for the target project.
TRIGGER when: user wants to start a new project, define requirements, create a project spec, decompose a project into components, scaffold a new application, set up project structure, analyze requirements, or extend an existing project with new features that need full planning.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to implement code (/shipwright-build), run tests (/shipwright-test), fix a bug or make a small change (/shipwright-iterate), deploy (/shipwright-deploy), generate a changelog (/shipwright-changelog), plan implementation details for an existing spec (/shipwright-plan), or design UI mockups (/shipwright-design).
- shipwright-design:design: Generate UI mockups from IREB specs as standalone HTML. Screens + user flows, iteratable via chat.
TRIGGER when: user wants to create UI mockups, design screens, generate HTML wireframes, create visual designs, design a user interface, preview a layout, create user flow diagrams, iterate on a screen design, or process design feedback.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to implement code (/shipwright-build), run tests (/shipwright-test), fix a bug or change code (/shipwright-iterate), deploy (/shipwright-deploy), create requirements (/shipwright-project), or plan implementation details (/shipwright-plan).
- shipwright-build:build: Implements code from /shipwright-plan sections using TDD, code review, Conventional Commits, and feature branch workflow.
TRIGGER when: user wants to implement a planned section, build code from a plan, implement a feature from a section file, start coding a planned component, continue building the next section, write test cases for a section, implement tests, or create unit tests from a plan.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to fix a bug or make a quick change to existing code (/shipwright-iterate), run existing tests (/shipwright-test), deploy (/shipwright-deploy), create requirements (/shipwright-project), plan implementation (/shipwright-plan), or design UI (/shipwright-design). Also DO NOT TRIGGER for ad-hoc code changes that don't reference a section file — use /shipwright-iterate instead.
- shipwright-deploy:deploy: Deploy to Jelastic (Infomaniak) with smoke test verification, rollback support, and Supabase migrations.
TRIGGER when: user wants to deploy, push to production, deploy to dev, deploy to staging, publish the application, rollback a deployment, or check deployment status.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to write code (/shipwright-build), run tests (/shipwright-test), fix a bug (/shipwright-iterate), create a changelog (/shipwright-changelog), create requirements (/shipwright-project), plan implementation (/shipwright-plan), or design UI (/shipwright-design).
- shipwright-changelog:changelog: Parses Conventional Commits from git history, generates Keep-a-Changelog entries, creates version tags, and opens PRs.
TRIGGER when: user wants to create a changelog, generate release notes, tag a version, create a release, bump version number, create a PR for release, or review unreleased changes.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to write code (/shipwright-build), run tests (/shipwright-test), fix a bug (/shipwright-iterate), deploy (/shipwright-deploy), create requirements (/shipwright-project), plan implementation (/shipwright-plan), or design UI (/shipwright-design).
- shipwright-compliance:compliance: Generates audit-ready compliance documentation from Shipwright pipeline data. Produces traceability matrix, test evidence, change history, SBOM, and dashboard with Mermaid diagrams.
TRIGGER when: user wants compliance reports, audit documentation, traceability matrix, test evidence report, SBOM, change history, or compliance dashboard. Also when user asks about regulatory compliance, audit readiness, or documentation for auditors.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to write code (/shipwright-build), run tests (/shipwright-test), fix a bug (/shipwright-iterate), deploy (/shipwright-deploy), create requirements (/shipwright-project), plan implementation (/shipwright-plan), or design UI (/shipwright-design).
- shipwright-iterate:iterate: Lightweight SDLC for ongoing changes in completed Shipwright projects.
TRIGGER when: user asks to add a feature, fix a bug, change behavior, refactor, update, modify, or improve code in a project that has shipwright_run_config.json with status complete. Also when user describes a bug report, enhancement request, or any code-level change to a finished project.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks about project setup (/shipwright-project), planning (/shipwright-plan), initial build (/shipwright-build), deployment (/shipwright-deploy), running tests (/shipwright-test), or non-code tasks like documentation questions. Also DO NOT TRIGGER when the pipeline is still in_progress — those changes belong to the current pipeline phase.
- shipwright-preview:preview: Start local dev server and show browser preview URL. Available after first build split.
TRIGGER when: user wants to see the app, preview changes, check the UI, open the browser, show me the app, start the dev server, or view the running application.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: user asks to write code, run tests, deploy, or any other SDLC phase.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
